block_01: As you already know, the promotion of IQ Option to users from the
block_05: based on the CySEC’s requirements. All traffic sources used to attract users from EEA countries have to be pre-approved and are regularly checked to ensure full compliance with the guidelines.
block_06: If your materials are found to be non-compliant, you may lose the ability to promote IQ Option to EEA countries or even get your affiliate account blocked altogether. Therefore, please review your EEA traffic sources to make sure they meet the requirements.
block_07: To help you understand what to look for first, we compiled a list of the most common issues:
block_08: Advertising trading instruments or features that are not available in EEA countries.
block_09: Make sure to determine the audience you plan to target with each of your traffic sources. If the material is intended for traders from EEA countries, it has to reflect the correct trading conditions available to them. Namely, do not promote binary options, higher leverages, trading tournaments, or VIP status in such materials. Be advised that using the materials with mixed information that targets both EEA and non-EEA audience is to be avoided.
block_10: Missing, incorrect, or not prominent risk warnings.
block_11: All promotions and invitations to open an account (registration links) must include a relevant risk warning that should be written in bold, in a text not smaller than the regular text of the material, in the language of the material, and in a place that would attract a reader’s eye to them:
block_12: CFDs are complex instruments and come with a high risk of losing money rapidly due to leverage.
block_13: 74-89% of retail investor accounts lose money when trading CFDs with this provider.
block_14: You should consider whether you understand how CFDs work and whether you can afford to take the high risk of losing your money.
block_15: For cases where there is not enough space, e.g., registration buttons, you can use the shortened risk warning:
block_16: 74-89% of retail investor accounts lose money when trading CFDs with this provider.
block_17: Using misleading statements or focusing on the benefits of a financial product or a service only.
block_18: A balance between a potential benefit and the relevant risk has to be maintained. Claims like “trading is easy/exciting”, “suitable for everyone/beginners”, “start winning/making/earning money”, “trading leads to success, a change in lifestyle, financial freedom” or any other claims with the similar promotional meaning must not be used.
block_19: Advertising IQ Option using traffic sources dedicated to certain topics.
block_20: Traffic sources that exclusively offer trading signals, social trading, copy trading, automated trading, robots, investment advice, portfolio management services, as well as gaming or gambling, cannot be used for the promotion in EEA countries.
block_21: Using outdated content and promo materials.
block_22: Most of the materials published a few years ago no longer reflect the current trading conditions and have to be updated. As for the promo materials, please use the up-to-date ones available in the
block_24: If you have any questions regarding your traffic sources, please let us know so that we can sort them out.
